input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to analyze the project for incremental build issues. Let me look at the project files to understand the build configuration.

1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, and it writes a file containing `DateTime.Now` on every build. This guarantees the output changes every time, which forces `CoreCompile` to re-run even when source code hasn't changed.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild can never skip it. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes a `.g.cs` file on every build. Even if the git hash hasn't changed, the file is unconditionally overwritten (updating its timestamp), causing recompilation.

---

### How to fix

**`GenerateTimestamp`** — Either remove it or gate it with `Inputs`/`Outputs` and avoid volatile data (`DateTime.Now`) in the content, since a volatile value always changes and invalidates the build.

**`EmbedGitHash`** — Add `Inputs`/`Outputs` and write the file only when the hash actually changes:

```xml
<Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"
        Inputs="$(MSBuildProjectFile)"
        Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs">
```

Plus, compare the new hash against the existing file content and skip the write if unchanged. This prevents the file timestamp from changing and avoids triggering recompilation.
